items.title: Thai cabinet approves plans to borrow $12bn to ease Iran war fallout
Mosca, cinque persone morte in un attacco in Crimea
items.description: Alla mezzanotte è scattata la tregua unilaterale di Kiev, attacco russo su Zaporizhzhia

items.title: Zelenskyy slams Russia as strikes kill 22 in Ukraine before announced ceasefire
items.description: Zelenskyy rebuked Moscow for what he said was its "utter cynicism" in launching the attacks after Russia announced a unilateral ceasefire over two days later this week.

items.title: In Gaza, the simplest of weddings are barely affordable
items.description: Couples marry in tents in Gaza as war, displacement, and soaring prices strip weddings of joy.

items.title: Wednesday briefing: How Trump’s attempt to reopen to strait of Hormuz brought war closer again
items.description: In today’s newsletter: ​The brief US effort to ​s​teer trapped vessels through the ​waterway ​put the fragile ceasefire under strainGood morning. Uncertainty once again reigns in the Middle East. The uneasy ceasefire between the US and Iran threatened to disintegrate after Donald Trump launched an initiative – dubbed “…

items.title: Russian Strikes Kill 22 in Ukraine Hours Before Kyiv-Proposed Ceasefire
items.description: The strikes came hours before a proposed ceasefire, with Zelensky condemning Russia’s “utter cynicism” in launching deadly attacks while seeking a brief truce for its May 9 Victory Day parade.

items.title: Iran threatens Hormuz shipping, as Trump said to shelve strikes amid diplomacy
items.description: Trump: ‘I’ll let you know’ when Iran breaks truce; Rubio says offensive operations against Iran over; UAE reports Iranian attacks for second day; Israel ‘closely monitoring,’ ready to ‘respond with force,' says IDF chief The post Iran threatens Hormuz shipping, as Trump said to shelve strikes amid diplomacy appeared fi…

items.title: Rubio says US military completed offensive operations in Iran
items.description: The United States said on Tuesday that it had ended offensive operations against Iran. But Washington warned it could unleash a “devastating” response to new threats in the Strait of Hormuz, as clashes and rival warnings from Tehran raised fears that a fragile ceasefire could collapse.

items.title: Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire already broken by Russian strikes
items.description: At least 28 people were killed in Russian strikes just before Ukraine's ceasefire was due to take effect on Wednesday, even as Kyiv accused Moscow of new violations hours later.

items.title: Strategic neutrality: How Syria is winning in the Iran war
items.description: Syria's new government has deliberately not become directly involved in the Iran war. It's presenting itself as neutral and as a possible solution to the Strait of Hormuz blockade.

items.title: UAE Accuses Iran Of Hitting Oil Sector, World Leaders Condemn Dangerous Escalation
items.description: Several countries have condemned Iran and called an attack on oil facilities in the United Arab Emirates a dangerous escalation. Thick smoke was filmed pouring out from the Fujairah Oil Industry Zone that the UAE claimed was hit by an Iranian drone attack on May 4. This comes as both the US and Iran fired shots in the …

items.title: How Colombia became a top exporter of mercenaries
items.description: Every year Colombia retires thousands of battle-hardened veterans from its armed forces. Many end up enlisting in foreign conflicts.

items.title: Education or food? Funding shortfalls force Sudanese refugees in Egypt to ‘make very difficult decisions’
items.description: Civilians who have fled the war in Sudan and sought shelter in neighbouring Egypt could potentially face a new battle – the loss of critical services that ensure their survival, the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, has warned.

items.title: U.K. Targets Russian Army Recruitment Network and Drone Suppliers in Latest Sanctions Package
items.description: The new measures target what British officials condemned as a “barbaric” network that lures foreign nationals to Russia with the promise of work, only to force them into frontline combat in Ukraine or military production.

items.title: Mali junta leader names himself defence minister after predecessor killed
items.description: Former Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in a massive offensive by combined jihadist and separatist forces.
